titlestmt.titleproper: Chicago, Ill. Garfield Park Congregational Church records, 1901-1914.
titlestmt.author: Zachary Bodnar

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday April 21, 2026 at 12:35

did.unittitle: Chicago, Ill. Garfield Park Congregational Church records, 1901-1914.
did.abstract: The Garfield Park Congregational Church was gathered in Chicago's West Garfield Park neighborhood in 1901. A church building was constructed in 1908. The church dissolved in 1919. This collection contains a single volume of church records which include membership records, baptismal records, Sunday school statistics, and the minutes of meetings.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to this collection is unrestricted and open to the public.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use
userestrict.p: Items in this collection are subject to U.S. Copyright Law. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine the copyright status of collection items and to secure any permissions necessary for their reproduction and publication. Requests for permission to publish material must be discussed with the archivist or librarian.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], in the Chicago, Ill. Garfield Park Congregational Church records, 1901-1914, RG5585. The Congregational Library & Archives, Boston, MA.

acqinfo.head: Acquisition Information
acqinfo.p: Materials were donated to the Congregational Library & Archives by the Illinois Conference of the United Church of Christ in December 2024; accession number 2024-46.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection was processed by Zachary Bodnar, April 2026, according to the rules of Describing Archives: A Content Standard.

bioghist.head: Historical Note
bioghist.p: The Garfield Park Congregational Church was gathered in Chicago's West Garfield Park neighborhood in 1901. In 1908, the church built a new church building at the corner of S Springfield Avenue and W Congress Street. The church dissolved in 1919. After the church's closure, the building became Hagro Anshe Wilno Synagogue. The building was demolished in the 1950s to make room for the Congress Expressway, now known as the Dwight D. Eisenhower Expressway.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains a single volume of church records which include membership records, baptismal records, Sunday school statistics, and the minutes of meetings dating back to the church's founding.
